---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Overstorey — a field guide to plant"
page-layout: full
toc: false
format:
  html:
    css: assets/extras.css
---

::: {.hero-canopy}

```{=html}
<div class="hero-grid">
  <div class="hero-copy">
    <p class="eyebrow">Overstorey · a field guide to <code>plant</code></p>
    <h1 class="hero-title">Plants competing<br>for light, resolved<br>down the canopy.</h1>
    <p class="hero-lede">
      <code>plant</code> integrates the demography of a size-structured
      population through a light environment its own individuals create —
      assimilation, growth, and mortality emerging from leaf-level
      physiology rather than fitted curves.
    </p>
    <div class="hero-actions">
      <a class="btn-sun" href="guides/first-individual.html">Run your first individual →</a>
      <a class="btn-ghost" href="theory/size-structured-pde.html">Read the theory</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Signature element: light attenuating through a leaf-area profile.
       Beer–Lambert decay; bars are canopy layers, opacity ∝ light. -->
  <figure class="hero-fig" aria-label="Light attenuating through a canopy profile">
    <svg viewBox="0 0 240 360" role="img" class="canopy-svg">
      <defs>
        <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
          <stop offset="0%"  stop-color="#f4cf72"/>
          <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#eef5ee"/>
        </linearGradient>
      </defs>
      <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="360" fill="url(#sky)" opacity="0.25"/>
      <g class="layers"></g>
      <line class="lightline" x1="120" y1="0" x2="120" y2="360"/>
    </svg>
    <figcaption>light fraction <span class="mono">I(z)/I₀ = e<sup>−k·L(z)</sup></span></figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<script>
(function () {
  const g = document.querySelector('.canopy-svg .layers');
  if (!g) return;
  const N = 22, k = 0.5, top = 20, bottom = 340, w = 240;
  let cumLAI = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
    const y = top + (bottom - top) * (i / N);
    cumLAI += 0.18;                       // leaf area accumulating downward
    const light = Math.exp(-k * cumLAI);  // Beer–Lambert
    const bw = 30 + 70 * light;           // wider where more light reaches
    const r = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg','rect');
    r.setAttribute('x', (w/2 - bw/2).toFixed(1));
    r.setAttribute('y', y.toFixed(1));
    r.setAttribute('width', bw.toFixed(1));
    r.setAttribute('height', '8');
    r.setAttribute('rx', '2');
    r.setAttribute('fill', '#2a6243');
    r.setAttribute('opacity', '0');
    g.appendChild(r);
    const reduce = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;
    if (reduce) { r.setAttribute('opacity', (0.25 + 0.7*light).toFixed(2)); }
    else {
      r.animate(
        [{opacity: 0, transform: 'translateY(-6px)'},
         {opacity: 0.25 + 0.7*light, transform: 'translateY(0)'}],
        {duration: 500, delay: 120 + i*55, fill: 'forwards', easing: 'ease-out'}
      );
    }
  }
})();
</script>
```

:::

::: {.grid .feature-row}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Mechanistic, not fitted
Growth and mortality fall out of a carbon budget — assimilation minus respiration and turnover — so trait effects propagate the way they do in a plant.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Emergent competition
Each individual shades those below it. The light environment is solved as a fixed point of the community, not imposed.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Built to be reproduced
Every page here is frozen against a pinned `renv` lockfile and a recorded package version. Re-run any result, get the same figure.
:::

:::

::: {.callout-tip}
New here? Start with [installation](guides/install.qmd), then [your first individual](guides/first-individual.qmd). Looking for function-level docs? They live in the [reference site](https://traitecoevo.github.io/plant/reference/).
:::
